perturbation.original_text: 1. First Offer for Development Opportunities. During the period beginning on the Effective Date and ending on December 31, 2018 (the "Development Term"), each Party (the "Offering Party") agrees to provide to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") a right of first offer with respect to any proposed offer or arrangement, which the Offering Party (or its affiliate) desires to accept, under which the Offering Party or one of its affiliates would acquire the ownership of real property in the Market Area (the "Development Property") on which a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort would operate (a "Development Opportunity"). If the Offering Party is required to offer the Receiving Party a Development Opportunity pursuant to this Section 1, the Offering Party must deliver written notice to the Receiving Party, together with reasonable due diligence information in the Offering Party's possession to enable the Receiving Party to evaluate the Development Opportunity (collectively, the "Offer Notice"). The Receiving Party will have ten (10) business days after receiving the Offer Notice to notify the Offering Party whether the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer for that Development Opportunity. If the Receiving Party and exercises its right of first offer hereunder, and: (a) if the Receiving Party is Playa, then Playa (or its affiliate) and Hyatt's affiliate shall negotiate in good faith the terms of a management agreement and related documents under which Playa (or its affiliate) would manage a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort on the Development Property (subject to a franchise agreement between Hyatt and the affiliate of Hyatt that would own the Development Property), provided that Hyatt's affiliate acquires the Development Property on terms acceptable to it within sixty (60) days after delivery of the Offer Notice, and (b) if the Receiving Party is Hyatt, then Playa or its affiliate shall negotiate in good faith the terms of a franchise agreement and related documents for the operation (and, if applicable, development) of the Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort on the Development Property, provided that Playa's affiliate acquires the Development Property on terms acceptable to it within sixty (60) days after delivery of the Offer Notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. First Offer for Development Opportunities. During the period beginning on the Effective Date and ending on December 31, 2018 (the "Development Term"), each Party (the "Offering Party") agrees to provide to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") a right of first offer with respect to any proposed offer or arrangement, which the Offering Party (or its affiliate) desires to accept, under which the Offering Party or one of its affiliates would acquire the ownership of real property in the Market Area (the "Development Property") on which a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort would operate (a "Development Opportunity"). If the Offering Party is required to offer the Receiving Party a Development Opportunity pursuant to this Section 1, the Offering Party must deliver written notice to the Receiving Party, together with reasonable due diligence information in the Offering Party's possession to enable the Receiving Party to evaluate the Development Opportunity (collectively, the "Offer Notice"). The Receiving Party will have ten (10) business days after receiving the Offer Notice to notify the Offering Party whether the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer for that Development Opportunity. If the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer hereunder, and: (a) if the Receiving Party is Playa, then Playa (or its affiliate) and Hyatt's affiliate shall negotiate in good faith the terms of a management agreement and related documents under which Playa (or its affiliate) would manage a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort on the Development Property (subject to a franchise agreement between Hyatt and the affiliate of Hyatt that would own the Development Property), provided that Hyatt's affiliate acquires the Development Property on terms acceptable to it within sixty (60) days after delivery of the Offer Notice, and (b) if the Receiving Party is Hyatt, then Playa or its affiliate shall negotiate in good faith the terms of a franchise agreement and related documents for the operation (and, if applicable, development) of the Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort on the Development Property, provided that Playa's affiliate acquires the Development Property on terms acceptable to it within sixty (60) days after delivery of the Offer Notice.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the word "and" between 'Receiving Party' and 'exercises' creates an ambiguity. It becomes unclear if the Receiving Party needs to both (1) receive the offer notice and (2) affirmatively exercise the right of first offer for the subsequent conditions (a) and (b) to apply. This lack of clarity directly contradicts the expected logical flow and creates a potential dispute point regarding what constitutes a valid exercise of the right of first offer, specifically in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. First Offer for Development Opportunities. During the period beginning on the Effective Date and ending on December 31, 2018 (the "Development Term"), each Party (the "Offering Party") agrees to provide to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") a right of first offer with respect to any proposed offer or arrangement, which the Offering Party (or its affiliate) desires to accept, under which the Offering Party or one of its affiliates would acquire the ownership of real property in the Market Area (the "Development Property") on which a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort would operate (a "Development Opportunity"). If the Offering Party is required to offer the Receiving Party a Development Opportunity pursuant to this Section 1, the Offering Party must deliver written notice to the Receiving Party, together with reasonable due diligence information in the Offering Party's possession to enable the Receiving Party to evaluate the Development Opportunity (collectively, the "Offer Notice"). The Receiving Party will have ten (10) business days after receiving the Offer Notice to notify the Offering Party whether the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer for that Development Opportunity. If the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer hereunder, and: (a) if the Receiving Party is Playa, then Playa (or its affiliate) and Hyatt's affiliate shall negotiate in good faith the terms of a management agreement and related documents under which Playa (or its affiliate) would manage a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort on the Development Property (subject to a franchise agreement between Hyatt and the affiliate of Hyatt that would own the Development Property), provided that Hyatt's affiliate acquires the Development Property on terms acceptable to it within sixty (60) days after delivery of the Offer Notice, and (b) if the Receiving Party is Hyatt, then Playa or its affiliate shall negotiate in good faith the terms of a franchise agreement and related documents for the operation (and, if applicable, development) of the Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort on the Development Property, provided that Playa's affiliate acquires the Development Property on terms acceptable to it within sixty (60) days after delivery of the Offer Notice.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "and" creates a material ambiguity, making it unclear whether the Receiving Party must both receive the Offer Notice *and* exercise the right of first offer for clauses (a) and (b) to apply. This directly conflicts with the original text, where the intention seems to be that both conditions must be met. The Contradicted text and the Changed text are located in the same location but the omission of "and" in the Changed Text makes the Changed Text have a contradiction to the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Dispute Resolution. (a) All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall to the extent possible be settled amicably by negotiation between the Parties within fifteen (15) days from the date of written notice by either Party of the existence of such dispute, and, failing such amicable settlement, shall be finally settled by arbitration under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce ("Rules"). To the extent there is any conflict between the Rules and the Federal Arbitration Act as it pertains to such arbitration, the Rules shall prevail. (b) The arbitration panel shall consist of: (i) one arbitrator in the event the aggregate damages sought by the claimant are stated to be less than Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000), and the aggregate damages sought by the counter-claimant are stated to be less than Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000); or 4 (ii) three arbitrators in the event the aggregate damages sought by the claimant are stated to be equal to or exceed Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000), or the aggregate damages sought by the counterclaimant are stated to be equal to or exceed Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000). Each arbitrator (1) shall have no fewer than ten (10) years' experience in the international hotel business, (2) shall be licensed to practice law in the United States, and (3) shall not be a person, or an affiliate of a person, who has any past, present or currently contemplated future business or personal relationship with either Playa, Hyatt or any of their respective affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Dispute Resolution. (a) All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall to the extent possible be settled amicably by negotiation between the Parties within fifteen (15) days from the date of written notice by either Party of the existence of such dispute, failing such amicable settlement, shall be finally settled by arbitration under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce ("Rules"). To the extent there is any conflict between the Rules and the Federal Arbitration Act as it pertains to such arbitration, the Rules shall prevail. (b) The arbitration panel shall consist of: (i) one arbitrator in the event the aggregate damages sought by the claimant are stated to be less than Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000), and the aggregate damages sought by the counter-claimant are stated to be less than Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000); or 4 (ii) three arbitrators in the event the aggregate damages sought by the claimant are stated to be equal to or exceed Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000), or the aggregate damages sought by the counterclaimant are stated to be equal to or exceed Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000). Each arbitrator (1) shall have no fewer than ten (10) years' experience in the international hotel business, (2) shall be licensed to practice law in the United States, and (3) shall not be a person, or an affiliate of a person, who has any past, present or currently contemplated future business or personal relationship with either Playa, Hyatt or any of their respective affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of "and" after "date of written notice by either Party of the existence of such dispute," makes it unclear whether the 15-day negotiation period is mandatory. The original text suggests negotiation *must* occur for 15 days. The modified text creates a contradiction, implying negotiation may not be required at all before proceeding to arbitration. This affects the applicability of clause (a) and thus the entire dispute resolution framework laid out in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Dispute Resolution. (a) All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall to the extent possible be settled amicably by negotiation between the Parties within fifteen (15) days from the date of written notice by either Party of the existence of such dispute, failing such amicable settlement, shall be finally settled by arbitration under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce ("Rules"). To the extent there is any conflict between the Rules and the Federal Arbitration Act as it pertains to such arbitration, the Rules shall prevail.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 5(a) mandated a 15-day negotiation period prior to arbitration. The changed text, by omitting "and" after "date of written notice by either Party of the existence of such dispute," creates ambiguity. This change makes it unclear whether the 15-day negotiation is mandatory, thus contradicting the original intent of the clause within Section 5(a) itself, leading to uncertainty about the dispute resolution process and whether negotiation must occur before arbitration.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Waiver of Non-compensatory Damages. In any action or proceeding between the Parties (including any arbitration proceeding) arising under or with respect to this Agreement or in any manner pertaining to the Hyatt All-Inclusive Resorts or to the relationship of the Parties under this Agreement, each Party hereby unconditionally and irrevocably waives and releases any right, power or privilege either may have to claim or receive from the other Party any punitive or exemplary damages, each Party acknowledging and agreeing that the remedies herein provided and other remedies at law or in equity will in all circumstances be adequate. Both Parties acknowledge that they are experienced in negotiating agreements of this sort, and have had the advice of counsel in connection with, and fully understand the nature of, the waiver contained in this Section 14.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Waiver of Non-compensatory Damages. In any action or proceeding between the Parties (including any arbitration proceeding) arising under or with respect to this Agreement or in any manner pertaining to the Hyatt All-Inclusive Resorts or to the relationship of the Parties under this Agreement, each Party hereby waives and releases any right, power or privilege either may have to claim or receive from the other Party any punitive or exemplary damages, each Party acknowledging and agreeing that the remedies herein provided and other remedies at law or in equity will in all circumstances be adequate. Both Parties acknowledge that they are experienced in negotiating agreements of this sort, and have had the advice of counsel in connection with, and fully understand the nature of, the waiver contained in this Section 14.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'unconditionally and irrevocably', the certainty of the waiver of non-compensatory damages is diminished. The modification creates an internal contradiction with the rest of the paragraph, which stresses the parties' understanding and agreement regarding the waiver. The changed term creates uncertainty about whether either party retains the option to later contest the waiver's enforceability. The waiving is not 'unconditionally and irrevocably' anymore, making it uncertain if a judge can or cannot grant punitive or exemplary damages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Waiver of Non-compensatory Damages. In any action or proceeding between the Parties (including any arbitration proceeding) arising under or with respect to this Agreement or in any manner pertaining to the Hyatt All-Inclusive Resorts or to the relationship of the Parties under this Agreement, each Party hereby waives and releases any right, power or privilege either may have to claim or receive from the other Party any punitive or exemplary damages, each Party acknowledging and agreeing that the remedies herein provided and other remedies at law or in equity will in all circumstances be adequate. Both Parties acknowledge that they are experienced in negotiating agreements of this sort, and have had the advice of counsel in connection with, and fully understand the nature of, the waiver contained in this Section 14.
perturbation.justification: The original text included the phrase 'unconditionally and irrevocably,' which clearly indicates a complete and permanent waiver of the right to claim punitive or exemplary damages. The changed text omits this phrase, thereby weakening the waiver and introducing ambiguity about whether the parties can later contest its enforceability. This directly contradicts the intent of the original clause which aimed to provide certainty and preclude any future claims for such damages. Since the Contradicted Text is the original text, the omission of the mentioned phrase causes a contradiction as now the waiver might not be enforced.
